Also a large dish, a bucket, decorated with a fine Bacchic mask, a candelabrum foot and other pieces of bronze furniture.

name: Le Grotticelle
region_db: Between Ventotene and S. Stefano, Pontine Islands
cargo_types:
amphorae; About four hundred pieces belonging to couches, bronze fittings from the legs and carved bone decoration (including female figures and animal heads)  from the bed and back of the couch. _x000D_
Other items include bronze fittings from chests and boxes. _x000D_
Also a marble base (for a statue), a marble basin, a wooden marline-spike. _x000D_
Coarse ware and fineware.

name: Ognina A
region_db: Near Syracuse, east coast of Sicily

name: Kizil Burun Column
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; blocks; marble:Eight Proconnesian marble column drums averaging about 3 feet tall and ranging in diameter from 5 to 5.5 feet. This incremental graduation suggests that the eight drums and one capital comprise a single column over 30 feet tall. Newly quarried stone as the drums are not fluted and still preserve traces of corkscrew-shaped hoisting grooves. A group of large rectangular marble blocks which probably represent architectural elements associated with the column. This area also yielded an interesting array of newly-quarried, roughly-finished marble objects such as a small hand basin, the pedestal for one of two larger basins, and at least 6 uninscribed grave stones or stele. More than a dozen marble blocks.; Undecorated, utilitarian cookware (plates, casseroles). Numerous Italian ceramics including cooking pans. Several types of typical Hellenistic fineware (moldmade bowls, grey ware, and black glaze cups, bowls, and salt cellars).Nine bronze handles.

name: Vele Orjule/Lussino
cargo_types: A life-size bronze statue of 'Apoxyomenos' was found as a solitary find. Supposedly a Hellenistic or Roman copy of the famous Greek C4th original. Dated by organic material found inside the statue.

name: Mazara del vallo
region_db: Between Sicily and Cap Bon, Tunisia
cargo_types:
amphorae; A bronze satyr was retrieved on two different occasions: the discovery of the left leg, caught up in the nets of a fishing boat from Mazara in 1997, was followed by the discovery of the body in March 1998,  missing the other leg and the arms, however. The figure had larger than lifesize dimensions and was more than two metres tall. The overall weight of the preserved parts is around 108 kilograms (96 for the body, 12 the detached leg). The young Satyr is caught in the act of dancing, with his arms outspread, possibly holding a cup and a thyrsus, the left leg slightly raised and the head turned to one side with hair flowing around._x000D_
His semi-feral nature is shown by his pointed ears.

name: Aghios Eustratios(?)
cargo_types: Bronze statue of Augustus riding a horse.Tthis is the only preserved life-size equestrian statue of him. He wears a heavy chiton and a mantle and holds the reins with his left hand.

name: Tyre F

name: Marmaris B
cargo_types: marble; marble:Louteria, similar in shape and concretion to those  in the Bodrum Museum.

name: Serçe Limani B
cargo_types:
amphorae; Minor cargo of small handle-less pots possibly for unguents, courseware jugs and black gloss ware. Twenty seven jars of uniform size and shape were found (heights vary between 8.2 and 9.3 cm.).  They may have originally contained unguents as the narrow mouth and carefully formed rim indicate their use for such valuable liquids. The jars may originate in Egypt._x000D_
This globular amphora has comparable parallels to ones from Pergamun and Tarsus, but enough differences exist to mark this one as unusual._x000D_
Millstones.

name: Knidos B

name: Cape Gelidonya B

name: Şile

Huge unfinished statue of an emperor (4.5m), a  large female bust that may date to the early C2nd AD. A sarcophagus lid, a stele and two bowls. _x000D_
There may be more marble buried in the sand.

name: Marathon
cargo_types: Famous bronze statue of a boy, perhaps the god Hermes. It is the work of the school of Praxiteles and is dated to 325-300 BC.

name: Artemision
cargo_types: Bronze works of art, including C5th male statue of Poseidon/Zeus brandishing a trident with his raised right hand. It is an original work of a great sculptor, possibly  Kalamis, and a C2nd horse and jockey. The Jockey's physiognomy and original black skin are those of an Ethiopian, but his hairstyle is Greek, which implies that he is of mixed heritage. He is most likely a professional jockey. The best interpretation is that the group was set up in a sanctuary to honour one or more victories in horse races. The large size of the monument and the high quality of the sculpture suggest commission by a royal or wealthy Greek aristocratic. Lots of pottery, two cups and a lamp (perhaps dating the site to ca.150 BC).

name: Palairos (?)
cargo_types: marble; columns; marble:A column of green cipollino 4.10m long and 0.37m and 0.57m in diameter was collected in the net of a fishing boat.

A large amount of fine and coarse pottery. _x000D_
A dozen glass vessels, possibly Alexandrian?_x000D_

A necklace, a ring and other jewellery. _x000D_
Ingots and lead items._x000D_

Pottery including Pompeian red plates, rimless platters, fine-wall goblets and two-handled and four-handled cups, as well as two pieces of eastern pottery. Glass cups, stacked in piles of 8 or 10, in six different colours.

name: Porto Novo
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; blocks; marble:Over an area of 15m x 7m, four column fragments between 2.2m and 4.8m long with a diameter of 1.5m; five blocks, one of which is large at 23.3 tons, all in an unfinished state from Luna . A total weight is estimated at 138 tons. Some badly finished slabs of veneer of coloured marble, from a variety of sources, Chemtou, Teos, Chios and Carystos,  possibly left over from previous journeys. Black and white quartz from Egypt. A large number of sets of stone-working tools.

name: Kyrenia

amphorae; 10,000 almonds, lying in clusters, indicating that they were carried in sacks. _x000D_
29 millstones with mason's marks including single Greek letters, but a great variety of size and types of finish, possibly from Cos.

name: Koppo
cargo_types: amphorae; Pottery and part of moulded glass bowl.

name: Cape Andreas F

name: No name

name: Mahdia
cargo_types:
marble; columns; marble:Seventy Attic marble columns, weighing about 205 tonnes, varying in size from 1.85m to 4.4m. Also bases, capitals and basins. Total weight probably about 250 tonnes._x000D_
Seven life-sized marble heads and busts among them the bust of Aphrodite, that had already been used as tondi._x000D_
At least four large craters decorated with reliefs showing Dionysiac processions._x000D_
Bases, altars, fountain rims, small Doric capitals etc that had obviously furnished one or more sanctuaries. Two of the inscriptions refer to the sanctuary of the paraloi in the Piraeus.; Full-size and miniature bronze sculptures, including a statue of a winged youth (Eros?) and a herm of Dionysus with the signature of Boethus of Chalchedon, both of which show traces of having been removed from their marble bases; vases and candelabra, other decorative pieces. _x000D_
Bronze couches inscribed with Greek numbers, showing that the ship was carrying at least 22 of them.

name: Cavalière

Black-gloss Campanian ware._x000D_
A group of Spanish pottery, jugs, a jar, two goblets in grey ware and 'six sombreros de copa'.

name: Jakljan Island
cargo_types: marble; sarcophagi; marble:Three sarcophagi with lids.

name: Punta Mazza
cargo_types: amphorae; Corinthian relief cups showing the exploits of Heracles, battle scenes, ritual scenes and hunting scenes.

name: Beauséjour
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; blocks; marble:Five blocks of Luna marble well buried in an area 8m x 3m. Different sizes 2.1m x 1.4m x 1.5m = 11.7tons; 1.48m x 1.04m x 0.28m = 1.14tons; 1.33m x 0.76m x 0.3m = 0.8tons; 3m x 1.26m x 0.68m = 6.83tons; 1.9m x 1.26m x 0.55m = 3.9tons. Small tonnage compared to St. Tropez or Porto Nuovo, but similar to Meloria. All blocks are inscribed with quarry marks.

name: Les Riches Dunes 5
cargo_types: marble; columns; blocks; marble:A partially worked block of Luna marble 2m x 1m x 0.4m = 3.3 tons buried in sediment.Twenty-one plaques of grey marble; all are broken, but polished on both sides,  the largest one measures 28cm x 20cm apart from a triangular one of 44.2cm x 55.5cm. Small white column 50.4cm long.An Ionic white marble base of plinth and drum in perfect condition.; The head of an ibis made of lead 12.5cm long. These finds suggest the ship was carrying the property of a private collector.

name: Ouest Embiez 1
cargo_types: amphorae; Principal cargo of colourless glass: irregular blocks of very good quality raw glass up to 25kg each with a total of at least 10 tons stacked in the centre of the boat. More than fifteen fragments of sheet glass, all rectangular except one circular piece with a diameter of 43cm (very rare). At least 2000 pieces of glassware, the majority of which are cylindrical goblets, with different types of bases, stacked together. The sheets of glass were definitely made on the Syrian-Palestinian coast between Acre and Haifa which was famous for its glass production (Pliny NH 36.45; Josephus BJ 11.188; Strabo 16,758). The other glass could have come from any Mediterranean area.

name: Porticcio
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; marble:Two 2m high marble statues representing the Roman Emperor Philip 1 of Arabia (ruled AD 244-249) and his wife Marcia Otacilia, in fragments but weighing about 600kg each.; African coarse ware and millstones. Sheets of glass and glass objects with a total weight of at least 230kg.

name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-mer 18
cargo_types: marble; blocks; marble:Seven blocks of Luna marble in disorder over an area 8m long. The main block measures 2.8m x 1.1m, the others are smaller (1.4m x 1.7m, 1.8m x 1.5m etc).

name: Saintes Maries-de-la-mer 21
cargo_types: marble; blocks; marble:Seven blocks of Luna marble, two of which are completely buried in sediment. The heaviest load of the three marble wrecks at Saintes Marie.

name: Saintes Maries-de-la-mer 22
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; blocks; marble:Six or seven blocks of Luna marble over an area 12m long, parallel to the beach. Two of the blocks are half cylindrical, i.e. part columns, 2.1m long. Three others are rectangular.

name: Punta del Serrone
cargo_types: amphorae; A bronze foot (45cm) was found in 1972, followed by another 150 pieces of bronze sculpture in 1992 in an area 15m x 10m. Another foot, two statues, one late Hellenistic male nude, another Augustan male in a toga (1.47m). Seven bronze heads are better conserved, two date to the C4th BC, one with a short beard to the Late Hellenistic period, another male head and two female heads to the early Imperial period, and a final male head to late Imperial times. This was either a ship carrying works of art belonging to a private collector or a cargo of metal ready for recasting.

name: Meloria C
cargo_types: marble; blocks; marble:Eleven large white marble blocks and probably a half-worked column, 170cm in diameter and 350cm long. Total tonnage about 50 tons. Possibly Luna marble?

name: Baia di Salinedda
cargo_types: amphorae; African red slipware and coarse ware. Eastern Mediterranean glass.

name: Coscia di Donna

A total of about 100m cubed, weighing 265-275 tons. Could be Luna marble but not certain. Small black and white marble chips, possibly from earlier voyages.

The right hand and lower forearm of a wooden and ivory statue. _x000D_

name: Skerki Bank wreck F

African ware. _x000D_
Bronze artefacts.

name: Skerki Bank wreck G

name: Skerki Bank wreck B
cargo_types: amphorae; Probably a lot of organic cargo, now disintegrated.

name: Camarina A
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; blocks; marble:Two giallo antico columns (others salvaged?), 6m long,  wedged in by sandstone blocks.; Plates and casseroles of black-rim ware. Two small bronze buckets with swan's head terminal handles and other bronze objects, including strigils. A samovar 25cm high decorated with theatrical masks just below the rim and a lion's head protome snout. A rare (perfume?) container with blue enamel inlay, 24cm high, decorated with vine leaves and trellis patterns.

name: Marzamemi A
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; blocks; marble:Main part of the cargo was fifteen grey-white marble blocks from Attica, in an area 30m x 8m. name: Capo Granitola A
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; blocks; marble:Over sixty blocks approximately 3m x 1m x 1m in 8 files. Some salvage appears to have taken place in antiquity. Total weight c. 350 tons. Proconnesus marble in trapezoidal form; chips of Parian and unidentified Asiatic marble and verde antico were found between the blocks, presumably the remains of previous voyages.; Small pieces of pottery.

name: Giardini Naxos
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; blocks; marble:Twenty-four  white-grey Aegean columns and thirteen green cipollino rectangular blocks, over an area of 17m x 5-6m, with an estimated weight of 95 tons. The columns are in a half-finished state, while the blocks show only a surface roughing-out. Loaded at the same time forming two levels inside the hull.

name: Isola delle Correnti
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; blocks; marble:350 tons of marble blocks, white with a bluish streak, probably Asiatic. One fragment of a worked architectural piece.; Some pottery

name: Capo Taormina

name: Capo Granitola D
cargo_types: marble; columns; marble:Corinthian and Ionic capitals and plinths in white marble with blue veins, probably Asiatic.

name: Dramont I

A total weight of 23 tons.; A complementary cargo of polished (pumice) stones, equivalent to 500 litres. Several hundred kilos of corundum, a very hard and heavy mineral probably from the island of Naxos.

name: La Maddalena

region_db: Off west coast of Lipari Island

name: Torre Sgarrata
cargo_types: marble; sarcophagi; blocks; marble:Eighteen roughed out sarcophagi and twenty-three large blocks and marble veneer weighing at least 160 tons, from Thasos. Alabaster from Asia Minor. Total tonnage possibly 250 tons.

name: Grado (Julia Felix)
cargo_types: amphorae; Bronze stands with lions’ paws and acanthus decoration. Glass, much of it waste glass, packed in a barrel which had disintegrated; some square bottles with the base mark C SALVI GRATI.

name: Punta Scifo A
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; marble:Pavonazzetto and white marble from Docimium and grey-white marble from Proconnesus: columns, basins, blocks, capitals, bases, tables and a statuary group of Cupid and Psyche, some roughed out, some half-finished and some finished. Consular dates of AD 197 and 200 cut into some of the columns. 150 tons were raised from an area 50m x 50m, 3m thick and in 2 sections (cf Marzamemi A).

name: Torre Chianca
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; marble:Five marble columns, 8-9m long of cipollino, lying approximately 50cm apart, still in the original position in which they were transported. Another 2 are still buried in sand.; A lead seal marked with a Greek monogram and a star.

name: San Pietro
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; sarcophagi; marble:Twenty-three half-finished sarcophagi,  some left double and some with lids in fine white, crystalline stone, maybe from Aphrodisias. Most of the shapes are rectangular, while nine are rounded at the ends. Total weight about 75 tons.; Tile fragments.

name: Palagruza B (Pupak Reef)
cargo_types: amphorae; Pottery, numerous sherds of clay mortaria and, uniquely for the Adriatic,  chamber pots (lasanua). South Italic services of 'orlo bifido' dishes with plates/lids and Pompeian red ware plates were also found.

name: Supetric
cargo_types: A large quantity of tegulae and imbrices suggests a shipwreck.

name: Atlit A
cargo_types: Parker notes Assyrian bronze helmets, Babylonian weights (in the form of swans), furniture and candelabras.

name: Acque Chiare
cargo_types: amphorae; At least 4 terracotta 'vaulting tubes', but these may not have been part of the cargo.

name: Agay A (Camp Long)
cargo_types: amphorae; At least 7 basalt grinding stones atnd 250 bronze ingots of slender form, 38-40cm long. A brass ingot and some copper vessels were possibly also part of the cargo.

name: Agde G
cargo_types: Tablets of litharge (red lead) clay were stacked together in an area 20 x 14m, representing about 100 tons.

name: Agde J
cargo_types: A group of lead ingots of Republican Spanish type. They bear the mould-marks L. PLANI.L.F.RUSSINI, SOC GARGILI T F ET M LAETILI ML and SOC.M.C. PONTILIENORUM.M.F..The ingot of L. Planius Russinius is exactly similar to 2 ingots found in the Mahdia wreck.

name: Agde K
cargo_types: A group of lead ingots, cast in oval mounds resembling the "Pinna nobilis" shell and inscribed with TT. Compare the ingots from the Cabrera B wreck.

name: Ajaccio
cargo_types: Yellow or green glazed ware pottery 'a stecca' (ribbed), imitating products of Pisa, commonly found in Liguria and less frequently in Corsica.

name: Albenga
cargo_types: amphorae; A variety of pottery: black-gloss ware, including Campanian A plates and 'imitation Campanian' bowls. Some is said to have been stacked between the amphorae.

name: Alcudia
cargo_types: 'A considerable number' of tin ingots is said to have been found. About 30cm tall, with a hole near the top, and weighing 10.5-11.5 kg. They were slightly alloyed with silver.

name: Antheor
cargo_types: Glazed ware similar to that of the wreck Ajaccio.

name: Bagaud B
cargo_types: Tin and iron ingots, some marked. According to one round inscription, the merchants were Celts, specifically Hypocelts who were assembled by a Greek named Heracleides for the coomercial exploration of tin.

name: Preveto
cargo_types: amphorae; Cups, pitchers and bowls. Many tiles.

name: Calanque de l'Ane
cargo_types: A cargo of tiles, tegulae and imbrices, still in rows.

name: Camarina B
cargo_types: A block of 73 North African oil lamps that had been carried in fabric sacks, and all were marked with the maker's mark STACTE as well as being decorated

name: Sud Lavezzi 5
cargo_types: Tegulae; ceramics, terra sigillata plates from N Italy etc

name: Cape Gelidonya A
cargo_types: amphorae; 34 flat four-handled copper ingots (25kg each) cast in the shape of an ox hide, several tin ingots, several raw bronze ingots and broken bronze tools (scrap axes, adzes, knives and a spade carried in wicker baskets)) destined to be melted down for re-use.

name: Carry-le-Rouet
cargo_types: blocks; marble:24 tons of local French limestone.

name: Dhokos
cargo_types: Most of the 1,381 objects recorded were vase fragments dating from the Early Helladic II  period, inlcuding saucers, deep and medium-depth bowls, pots, jugs and plates. Fragments of a pyxis, a cylindrical or spherical box with a lid were also found. Larger vases included several wide-lipped earthenware jars and a pithos. Grinding wheels, fragments of lead ingots and obsidian blade shafts were also found.

name: Dramont A
cargo_types: marble; blocks; marble:Stone blocks measuring 4 x 1 x 1m

name: Dramont C
cargo_types: amphorae; Iron bars and blocks of resin; 3 millstones. Numerous ceramics, ollae, olpes, jars, plates, cups and some copper tableware.

name: Dramont E
cargo_types: amphorae; 30 tubuli of which 22 were intact may have been  part of the cargo? 800-900 plates of terra sigillata D  (Hayes 61B, Hayes 64, Hayes 65 and Hayes 50 B), some with geometric motifs, others botanical.

name: Dramont G
cargo_types: Tegulae and imbrices from Frejus, cf Bartelemy B and Roches d'Aurelle wrecks.

name: Favaritx
cargo_types: Hundreds of bronze plates, keys, chains, candelabra, statuettes, balances and coins - Christian liturgical objects,

name: Giens A/Redonne B1
cargo_types: amphorae; Campanian A ceramics - Lamboglia 27Ba cups with palms et 27c with rosettes as well as small cups L34a.

name: Giglio Campese A
cargo_types: amphorae; Attic and Corinthian ceramics, circular ingots (80cm diameter).

name: Gusteranski
cargo_types: amphorae; South Italian Pompeian ware consisting of 'orlo bifido' bowls, with plates/lids and standard Pompeian red plates, which were only 15% of the total of 50 brought to the surface.

name: Ilovik
region_db: between Sv. Petar isl and Kozjak island

name: Isis
cargo_types: amphorae; Common ware pottery, mostly Tunisian, datable to c AD 400, some are unique in style. A basalt millstone from Libya with a concave hopper and protruding handles is an previoulsy unknown form.

name: Ladispoli A
cargo_types: amphorae; A wooden head of a duck as decoration for a kline.

name: Les Laurons B
cargo_types: amphorae; Wheat - grains were preserved in pitch

name: Lavezzi A (1)
cargo_types: amphorae; At least 7 lead ingots and 18 copper ingots.

name: Ma'agan Mikha'el
cargo_types: Seventy Cypriot and East Greek clay vessels, including a pithos, basket-handled jars, oil lamps, small jugs,mortaria and a cooking pot. 12 tons of rocks, mainly blueschist from Euboea, as well as some magmatic from southern Cyprus.

name: Mal di Ventre
cargo_types: amphorae; 1100 Spanish lead ingots mined from Cartagena, each weighing about 100 Roman pounds and inscribed on theupper side with the manufacturer's mark. One was SOCMPONTILIENORUMMAE, ie Societas Marci et Caio Pontilienorum figli di Marco. The other LCARULILFHISPALIMN, ie. Luci Caruli Luci fili Hispali MeNenia tribu.

name: Marzamemi B
cargo_types: A cargo of pre-fabricated marble architectural pieces from a Byzantine church, including 28 bases and 27 capitals of Proconnesian marble, and an 'ambo' with 2 staircases from which a priest would deliver sermons.

name: Mateille A
cargo_types: A bronze consignment of female statue fragments, animal terminals for furniture, boar's tusk decoration, pendants, dishes and pots.

name: Minorca
cargo_types: amphorae; Bronze ingots weighing 80kg each, still arranged in rows.

name: Plemmirio A
cargo_types: Bronze lamps, censers, pitchers, bowls and a steelyard.

name: Port-Miou (Port Miou B)
cargo_types: amphorae; Sigillata D or African redslip, Hayes 81 B and 67 B 33

name: Port Vendres C/ 3
cargo_types: amphorae; A small amount of glass tableware.

name: Port Vendres E
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; marble:Five unfinished sheets of Luna marble.

name: Porticello
cargo_types: amphorae; At least two dozen lead ingots, looted, only 2 have been excavated that are stamped, probably from Laurion. Eight small clay inkpots. Bronze sculpture: a superb male bearded  head, approximately life size, cast in 2 sections; a fragment of right leg and buttock with drapery; a large fragment of drapery; a left hand; the rear protion of a left foot; right foot and leg; genitals; left buttock and thigh; right buttock and upper thigh; other miscellaneous fragments - in total, probably at least one draped male and two undraped males.

name: Punta del Diavolo
cargo_types: marble; columns; blocks; marble:A cargo of stone (marble??) comprising of blocks, slabs, small columns and pillars. It is covered in in concretions which hinders exact definition of the material, but is i  its original stacked position

name: Roches d'Aurelle
cargo_types: amphorae; Tegulae and imbrices from Frejus, cf Bartelemy B and Dramont G wrecks.

name: Saint Gervais B (2)
cargo_types: amphorae; Wheat - a layer of pitch spilt from the amphorae glued  this rich archaeological layer of organic material  in position in the back one third of the ship.

name: Saint Gervais C
cargo_types: amphorae; 11 glass ungentaria, 10 of which were found in a basket; an ESA plate;

name: Salakta
cargo_types: marble; columns; marble:Architrave and pilaster parts.

name: Saturo A
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; marble:Limestone blocks.; Campania A black-gloss pottery.

name: Secca dei Mattoni
cargo_types: amphorae; Ceramics mainly consist of cups of the Morel 2323 and 1222 type, Lamboglia 3 vases and ciboria (Morel 7450 and 7550 types), other forms such as the Lamboglia 5 (low bowls without handles) small Lamboglia 10 urns and two spindle-shaped ointment jars.

name: Serçe Limani A
cargo_types: Two tons of raw glass and one ton (between half a million and a million sherds) of broken glass (broken prior to loading on board and possibly stored in baskets as they were found in clumps) as well as 80 intact glass vessels were found. The number of vessels present may have been as high as 10-20,000., They include cups, plates, dishes, bowls, bottles, ewers, jugs, jars, lamps, cupping glasses, alembics and stemmed vessels, all of an Islamic origin.

name: Sud-Lavezzi B (2)
cargo_types: amphorae; 95  lead ingots arranged in 11 rows all bearing the the mould-mark MINVCIORVM and the counter stamp AP.IVN and ZETH and graffiti indicating their weight (51-54kg). 237 circular copper ingots were stacked in piles fore and aft of the lead (20kg), bearing 20 different inscriptions, but most commonly Q. C. ANTO.19 pieces of  Arretine pottery from 8 different objects - 1 small cup stamped with CERES[IM] / P CORNE , Chresimus was one of 30 slaves known to have belonged to P. Cornelius of Arezzo. Other small fragments of fineware and some coarseware.

name: Tour du Castellas 1
cargo_types: amphorae; A bronze Etruscan tripod dated to the beginning of the C5th BC.

name: La Tour Fondue
cargo_types: amphorae; Massiliot ceramics - cups and millstones.

name: Ulu Burun
cargo_types: amphorae; Ten tons of Cypriot copper ingots (354 oxhide ingots and 121 smaller bun- and pillow-shaped ingots). Nearly a ton of tin ingots. Raw ivory (elephant tusk and hippo teeth!) and ivory carvings, a gold goblet, ebony, glass ingots and an assortment of gold jewellery. Huge amount of organic material including fruits, nuts, spices and other plants  . Bronze tools and weapons Cypriot pottery

name: Cala Piomba
cargo_types: amphorae; Two lead ingots of trapezoidal shape

name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 2
cargo_types: amphorae; Iron ingots and 146 bars some of which are very well preserved with stamps EROTIS and LEPIDI, plus symbols of anchors and other unidentifiable oval shapes. Some measure as long as 123cm and weigh 6.5kg. The total number may have been as high as 3000 to 4000.

name: La Giraglia
cargo_types: amphorae; Six fragments of Aretine ware, one with a stampTHALI. Four fragments of blue-green glass from 2 plates and 2 cups.; amphora_type:Dr. 2-4 Tarraconensis

name: Marsa -El-Brega Byzantine
cargo_types: amphorae; Fineware - Hayes Late Roman C, Form 3, Type H - cooking pots and a sherd of African redslip ware

name: Point Iria
cargo_types: amphorae; 25 pieces of Mycenaean and Cypriot pottery, and Cretan transport jars. "Cypriot pithoi and LH/LM IIIB 2 pithoid jars) and medium size ones (Cretan stirrup-jars, Cypriot jugs and an amphora with incised linear signs)."  "a few decorated Mycenaean vases in fine ware (a deep bowl krater and one or two deep bowls)"

name: Tanit
cargo_types: amphorae; Timber, most probably cedars from Lebanon, bundles of wool and potter's clay.

name: Elissa
cargo_types: amphorae; Timber, most probably cedars from Lebanon, bundles of wool and potter's clay.

name: Tektaş Burnu
cargo_types: amphorae; Pottery appears to have been made in Ionia or on Chios, including a modest assortment of 2 handled drinking cups, one-handled bowls, table amphorae, oil lamps, a jug, a water pitcher and an askos. Fineware is limited to a stamped blak-glazed Attic kantharos and 2 small perfume vessels (a black-glazed askos and a creamy white stone alabastron.

name: Tantura A/Dor J
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; marble:A fragment of pavement from Trench IV and 2 slabs and 4 small fragments from Trench VI.; Plenty of organic material.

name: Tantura B/Dor N
cargo_types: Numerous remains of basketry, some of it amazingly delicate. Ceramics. Egyptian??

name: Tantura C
cargo_types: amphorae; Coarsewares, including perfectly preserved jugs.

name: Les Sorres X
cargo_types: amphorae; A few fragments of bowls.

name: Le Grand Ribaud F (A??)
cargo_types: amphorae; Etruscan and Greek black-figure ceramics and bronze drinking bowls.

name: Pointe Lequin 1A
cargo_types: amphorae; Small bronze statue of a helmeted soldier; a large amount of Attic coarse ware and B2 cups (about 2,500 pieces) - decorated with scenes of  battle, mythology and daily life; oil lamps

name: L'Isolella
cargo_types: Tegulae (57 complete and 388 fragments) and imbricae (56 complete and 80 fragments).

name: Pigne (Salerno)
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; marble:Over a hundred marble closed pine cones.; Glazed ceramic vessels

name: Ottiolu
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; marble:Finished grey, fine-grained marble slabs, possibly from Luna.

name: Cala Reale
cargo_types: amphorae; A shipment of glass paste tesserae accompanied the main cargo of amphorae.

name: L'Asinara 2
cargo_types: amphorae; A large concretion of minerals or metals.

name: Cala del Falco 1
cargo_types: amphorae; In the late '70s a 4m long lead bar was still visible .

name: Capo Galera
cargo_types: amphorae; Glazed pottery - bowls, plates and casseroles

name: Les Mouettes
cargo_types: amphorae; Tiles, copper ingots and lead ingots some of which are stamped M AURELI; a bronze tripod

name: Les Pierres
cargo_types: 75 blocks of stone from Bois des Lens, north-west of Nimes, including 7 columns and capitals. The blocks were still lying in alignment and were rectangular; the dimensions varied between 0.7 0.75 x 0.6m and 2.85 x 0.6 x0.7m. This type of stone was popular for its similar look to marble.

name: Les Tambours
cargo_types: columns; marble:Sixteen drums measuring between 0.5 and 0.8m high with traces of inscriptions, possibly from Les Lens

name: Bonnieu 5
cargo_types: marble; blocks; marble:Three rows of 57 finished limestone blocks

name: Les Riches Dunes 4
cargo_types: amphorae; Two bronze statues: the first is of a young naked child, 65cm high, possibly Eros, datable to C1st BC- AD. The second is also of a child, in a short tunic, 80cm high and very well preserved, datable to the same era. They are both probablly from Campania and may have belonged to an Italian moving to Gaul (?). A mosaic  emblema measuring 48 x 49cm representing the judgement of Marsyas on a base of travertine.

name: Calanque de Conca
cargo_types: amphorae; A large amount of fragments of jugs.

name: Marina di Fiori
cargo_types: amphorae; A secondary cargo of blocks of minerals, never found on a wreck before. Some are of clay and are stamped M.ACILI and VENU

name: Erbalunga
cargo_types: 32kg of medium and small-sized coins dated to the end of the reign of Constantine I and the end of the C4th AD. The amphora was a Byzantine Keay XXV.

name: Pointe Cacalu
cargo_types: amphorae; Oil lamps including Dressel-Lamboglia type 3 classic.

name: Les Moines 1
cargo_types: Some decorated fragments of Arabic ceramics and tableware, some from Pisa

name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 6
cargo_types: Iron ingots and bars, some stamped. Apiece of Campanian pottery was found in one of the concretions dating the wreck to the second half of the C1st BC

name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 8
cargo_types: amphorae; Twenty concretions of iron ingots

name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 10
cargo_types: A concretion 8m long of iron ingots with illegible stamps.

name: Plage d'Arles 5
cargo_types: Sigillata from La Graufesenque, some stamped, that dates the wreck to the period of Claudius/Vespasian.

name: Sud Caveaux 1
cargo_types: amphorae; Ceramics found at the far end of the wreck

name: Les Battuts 4
cargo_types: amphorae; A three-beaked bronze lamp, very rare.

name: Pointe de la Voile
cargo_types: amphorae; Campanian B ware form L5a

name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 4
cargo_types: A large number of bronze objects - tableware, furniture pieces, jewellery and a statuette possibly of Mercury. Bronze scales, measuring 26cm and engraved with numbers I-VII on one side, with hooks decorated as ducks' heads; a few other bronze decoration fragments including part of a 'simplum' and a hinged bronze lid decorated with a dolphin.

name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 24
cargo_types: Large iron bars weighing between 23 and 33kg

name: Cavalaire 1
cargo_types: A huge amount of weapons - at least 8 cannons, projectile weapons, crossbow parts, lance tips

name: Barthelemy B
cargo_types: Tegulae and imbrices from Frejus cf Dramont G and Roches d'Aurelle wrecks.

name: Lardier 4
cargo_types: Nine rows of tegulae in 2 layers and imbrices in the residual spaces. A large amount of pottery typical of the lower Argens valley (Rivet form 17)

name: Grand Rouveau 2
cargo_types: amphorae; Tiles, between 100 and 300 imbrices and probably as many tegulae.

name: Pabuç Burnu
cargo_types: amphorae; blocks; A heavy concentration of grape seeds in the lower region of the site suggests that the ship also carried a bulk cargo of grapes or raisins, perhaps packed in sacks or baskets, which did not survive underwater.

name: Marina Grande di Bacoli
cargo_types: amphorae; Numerous fragments of unidentified pottery.

name: Punta Licosa 2
cargo_types: marble; blocks; marble:About 15-20  blocks with dimensions varying from 50 to 100cm which have clearly been squared

name: Capo Bianco
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; blocks; marble:Blocks of heterogeneous dimensions and drums of columns, distributed in a chaotic manner among the rocks. One of the larger blocks had been worked to create a semi-column on one of the long sides. The marble is of 3 different types.

name: Capo Donato
cargo_types: Cooking ware, generally of an open-form type.

name: Capo Colonna, Cicala
cargo_types: marble; columns; blocks; marble:Blocks of considerable dimensions, 6-7m in length, including 2 coumn drums with a diameter of c70cm and a length of 4-5m. The blocks laid as headers and sitting on their long sides are stacked without any particular order and are mostly parallelepiped or cubed in form and all are semi-worked and carved.

name: Cantafora B
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; blocks; marble:An accumulation of semi-worked blocks distributed in a disorderly manner and slightly overlying one another. Several of the column drums, with a diameter of 70cm, are already prepared with the hole for fixing the the column cut in the centre of the circumference.

name: Isole Pedagne Grandi
cargo_types: amphorae; marble; columns; marble:Various broken pieces of an unidentified column.

name: Hahotrim C
cargo_types: marble; columns; blocks; marble:'Masonry', capitals and columns

name: Ashkelon
cargo_types: marble; columns; marble:'Masonry' columns and drums.

name: Tantura F
cargo_types: amphorae; About 30 ceramic vessels, some containing fish remains, and a juglet. A large amount of fish bones, carobs and olive stones were also found on the site.

name: Tantura E ? Trench IX
cargo_types: amphorae; Two ashlar blocks are retained in their original alignment, directly inboard of the transverse timber. Maybe cargo, alternatively they may have served as ballast.

name: Dor 2001/1
cargo_types: amphorae; Almost all of the hull remains were covered by about 100 building stones made of local kurkar (coarse calcerous sandstone) stacked in 2 layers of 3 to 7 rows.The stones are approximately the same size, 57 x 28 x 18cm.

name: Dor south-east
cargo_types: Two piles of kurklar (ashlar) stones measuring 24 x 25 x 60cm, some of which have a rectangular slot. Metal artefacts.

name: Ashkelon North Roman
cargo_types: Bronzes: 3 figurines, a nude female (20cm, Aphrodite?), a naked bearded man (10cm, a Satyr?)  and a small zoomorphic  figure (1.9cm, monkey?). Other bronzes: a bell (12cm), a weight in the shape of a cardium shell, 2 decorative pieces, a lid of a vessel that bears 2 busts in relief and a lead mace head.

name: Herzliya Beach
cargo_types: amphorae; A cargo of unfinished donkey millstones made of basalt - 6 conical lower stones (0.8 x 0.85 m, 395kg) and 5 biconical upper stones (0.6 x 0.57m, 250kg) - were scattered along the flat rocky seabed.

name: Çamalti Burnu 1
cargo_types: amphorae; Other types of storage jars and two or three dozen broken anchors destined to be repaired or used as scrap iron

name: Levanzo I
cargo_types: amphorae; Numerous tubi fitilli (vaulting tubes), at least 100 of which were visible on the surface. Shape and sizes are consistent, c. 13.5 x 5.5 cm but  they are crudely formed and produced from poor-quality clay.

name: Lerici
cargo_types: marble; columns; marble:A cargo of columns.

name: Caprara
cargo_types: amphorae; Two piles of tiles still in stacked position. The tiles have a form typical of the Roman period, with the dimensions of approximately 40 cm x 30 cm, with a flat surface and raised edges on the longer two sides. The concretions which cover this material mean it is impossible to identify any stamps.

name: Bateguier/ Le Batiguier
cargo_types: amphorae; An extensive amount of pottery - ewers, water jugs, dishes etc. decorated with a great variety of designs, sometimes with Arabic graffiti. A very rare lamp with 7 openings. Millstones. Copper cauldrons. They seemed to have been transported in large earthenware jars without handles, decorated with digitated friezes.

name: Maguelone
cargo_types: 12 copper ingots and an iron bar stamped with HAEDI cf Saintes-Maries-de-la-mer 2.

name: Les Moines 3
cargo_types: amphorae; Three lead ingots (type Domergue 1)were found nearby.

name: Est Perduto 2
cargo_types: amphorae; A large amount of Campanian ceramics - plates, bowls and cups.

name: Mazarron 1
cargo_types: amphorae; Urns, baskets, mortars, religious objects and carafes.

name: Alonnisos
cargo_types: amphorae; High quality black-glazed pottery, the best of which came from Athens, including skyphoi, cups decorated with petals, drinking vessels. Heavy terracotta mortars with 2 handles, a large base and a thick rounded lip. Bowls with black-glazed bases from southern Italy.

name: Ekinlik Adasi Marble wreck
cargo_types: marble; columns; marble:At least 17 columns, some broken, presumably from Proconnesus.

name: Ashkelon North Byzantine
cargo_types: Handles of lead and bronze vessels soje with incised designs. Dozens of bronze coins of the 40 numia denomination of the Constantinople and Nicomedia mints, bearing the bust of Justin and his wife Sofia.

name: Tel Ashqelon
cargo_types: Lead, including dozens of loaf-shaped ingots (80-100 kg each). Most bear inscriptions that have yet to be deciphered. Next to the ingots were 3 elongated pieces of iron (8 x 10 x 50cm) whose function is unclear.

name: Le Formiche di Capraia
cargo_types: amphorae; Black-glazed pottery from central Italy, including plates, cups and bowls.

name: Siciliano (Porto San Paolo)
cargo_types: amphorae; Ceramics: Ostia III 267 and Ostia I 17 plates

name: Rena Maiore
cargo_types: Lead ingots, many of them stamped.

name: Olbia (statue)
cargo_types: amphorae; Fragments of a terracotta statue of Hercules.

name: Capo Passero II
cargo_types: Lead ingots probably from Spain each weighing about 30kg and stamped with the manufacturer's mark

name: Scauri
cargo_types: amphorae; Ceramics - cylindrical pots, bowls and cups; glass bottles and beakers in fragments. Oil lamps and some Sigillata Africana D

name: Kuçuk Ada
cargo_types: Terracotta pipes  in 2 piles 38.6 x 5.1m and 26.3 x 20.4m at the 2 different depths.

name: Atlit North Bay I
cargo_types: Decorated bronze handles of large vessels.

name: Nahal Oren
cargo_types: Bronze furniture/works of art? - bronze attachments of a bed with remains of wood, a stylized bronze handle of a container and  adecorated gold plaque.

name: Mandriola A
cargo_types: amphorae; Many fragments of African table ware

name: Cala Su Pallosu
cargo_types: amphorae; Millstones - 4 lower cone-shaped parts (metae) and 5 upper parts (catilli). Glass. Cups.

name: Qefalit Tile wreck
cargo_types: amphorae; The tile cargo is still stacked and forms an interlocking and compacted deposit. Pan and cover tiles are present of Corinthiam manufacture measuring 29 x 20 inches.

name: La Manga (Bajo de la Campana)
cargo_types: amphorae; A cargo of ivory, amber and many ceramic objects. The 10 ivory tusks measure between 70 and 150 cm long and are inscribed with Phoenician writing. They have come from a race of elephants now thought to be extinct. Also on board were 28 small tin ingots, 2 copper ingots, hundres of galena (lead ore) nuggets) and stones containing silver and lead, and plates, bowls, several double-sided wooden combs, ivory knife handles, bronze needles and chandeliers.

name: Pseira
cargo_types: amphorae; Ceramics including jugs, cups and cooking pots.

name: Polyaigos (marble)
cargo_types: marble; columns; marble:A single column of cipollino from Carystos was found that may come from an otherwise unknown wreck transporting architectural elements.

name: Megalo Stironisi
cargo_types: A cargo of tiles in sets of 6 and tied in large piles.

name: Cala Sant Vicents 2
cargo_types: amphorae; Bundles of iron tools, some tin ingots, baskets and grain-rubbers.

name: Boka Kotorska  1
cargo_types: Pan tiles and cover tiles are in several stacks, many of which are now postitioned vertically. The pan tiles measure c 65 x 45 cm with flanges angled at 90 degrees and are characteristic of Roman manufacture during the Imperial period.

name: Bozburun tile wreck
cargo_types: amphorae; Roof tiles, both cover and pan: over 10 of red fabric with white inclusions have been exposed possibly by an octupus (!). The cover tiles are about 1m long and 0.2-0.3m  wide, and slightly rounded to form a convex shape that is more acute at their edges than at the centre, stacked one upon the other and lying on their sides.

name: Les Catalans 2
cargo_types: marble; columns; marble:The remains of 2 white marble statues.  One of the statues (46cm high) ressembles an Apollo although there is no head, datable to mid C1st AD, whilst the other is simply part of a female foot with a sandal. A great number of unfinished architectural pieces - columns, architraves, capitals, slabs - may or may not be from the same wreck.; The small bronze statue represents Apollo as well and is 12cm high, found 138m away from the marble statues. It is of mediocre quality and is datable to the Roman Imperial period.

name: Aci Trezza 2
cargo_types: A cargo of millstones, of which 20 have been recovered and are now exhibited at Naxos Antiquarium.

name: Isola Rossa 3
cargo_types: amphorae; A concretion probably of metal

name: Skerki Bank Keith Reef 3
cargo_types: amphorae; Seven mortaria, a rare find in late Roman wrecks, cf Ustica wreck

name: Skerki Bank Keith Reef 4
cargo_types: Pieces of raw glass, yellow, green and blue in colour, and weighing between 24 and 126gm. Analysis suggests that are ancient since they consist of sodium silicate which is typical of Roman glass.

name: Skerki Bank Keith Reef 5
cargo_types: At least 5 lead ingots, weighing between 20 and 25kg, some of which bear stamps.

name: Cape Glavat/Mljet B
cargo_types: amphorae; Southern Italian pottery, 'orlo bifido' dishes with the plates/ lids (more than 500) and 15 Pompeian red plates. Great quantities of rough glass clumps.

name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-mer 9
cargo_types: Hundreds of iron ingots of differing sizes in 4 rows and at least 2 layers in concreted masses, some stamped. A piece of ceramic?

name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-mer 11
cargo_types: Iron ingots amalgamated with fragments of amphorae.

name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-mer 23
cargo_types: Iron ingots in mass concretions, 3m x 2m

name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 25
cargo_types: amphorae; About 50 tons of iron bars and ingots in concretion.

name: Camarina bronzes
cargo_types: amphorae; Bronze objects including oil lamps, bronzes similar to those found in the noble houses of Pompeii and Herculaneum, decorative pots, braziers, chandeliers and parts of statuettes of Mercury.
